ucs.title: [LLM] Volt Typhoon (Vanguard Panda) NTDS.dit theft via ntdsutil 'ifm create full' on domain controller
ucs.description: Hunts the signature ntdsutil 'Install From Media' invocation Volt Typhoon uses to extract NTDS.dit from a compromised DC without dropping malware — documented by CISA AA24-038A and Microsoft's Volt Typhoon writeup. Pure LOLBin, no SHA to track, so command-line shape is the entire detection.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=ntdsutil.exe OR Processes.original_file_name=ntdsutil.exe) (Processes.process="*ifm*" OR Processes.process="*create full*" OR Processes.process="*ac i ntds*" OR Processes.process="*activate instance ntds*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(process, "(?i)(ifm|create\s+full|ac\s+i\s+ntds|activate\s+instance\s+ntds)") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"           // exclude machine accounts
| where FileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe"
   or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe"
   or ProcessCommandLine has "ntdsutil"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("ifm", "create full", "ac i ntds", "activate instance ntds", "create full c:\\")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentAccount = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          IsRemoteSession = AdditionalFields,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688              // process create — requires audit policy + cmd-line auditing GPO
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\ntdsutil.exe"
   or CommandLine has "ntdsutil"
| where CommandLine has_any ("ifm", "create full", "ac i ntds", "activate instance ntds")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType, SubjectUserName
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Volt Typhoon NTDS.dit Theft via ntdsutil IFM
id: 7c9e3a52-volt-ntdsutil-ifm
status: experimental
description: Detects ntdsutil.exe invoked with 'ifm create full' arguments — Volt Typhoon's signature LOLBin technique to extract Active Directory NTDS.dit from a domain controller without malware.
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-038a
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/05/24/volt-typhoon-targets-us-critical-infrastructure-with-living-off-the-land-techniques/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.003
  - detection.threat_hunting
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith: '\ntdsutil.exe'
  selection_cmd:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'ifm'
      - 'create full'
      - 'ac i ntds'
      - 'activate instance ntds'
  condition: selection_image and selection_cmd
falsepositives:
  - Authorized DC backup/migration via ntdsutil — confirm with change ticket and source host (should be a DC, run by tier-0 admin)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Volt Typhoon (Vanguard Panda / VOLTZITE / DEV-0391 / Bronze Silhouette / UNC3236) deliberately avoids implants and instead uses native Windows tooling. ntdsutil 'ifm create full' on a DC is uniquely listed in CISA AA24-038A as part of the actor's documented playbook — legitimate DC operators virtually never run this interactively (a backup happens via NTBackup / DFSR / dedicated backup agent). Pai
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Volt Typhoon (VOLTZITE) netsh portproxy relay setup chained with discovery LOLBins
ucs.description: Hunts the netsh interface portproxy relay-creation step Volt Typhoon uses on compromised edge hosts to pivot from internet-facing devices into critical-infrastructure internal networks, optionally enriched with the actor's stereotyped on-host discovery chain (tasklist/netstat/net group/systeminfo/ipconfig). CISA AA24-038A and Microsoft both single out this exact pattern.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=netsh.exe Processes.process="*portproxy*" (Processes.process="*add v4tov4*" OR Processes.process="*add v4tov6*" OR Processes.process="*add v6tov4*" OR Processes.process="*add v6tov6*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [ | tstats summariesonly=t count as discovery_events values(Processes.process_name) as discovery_bins from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN (cmd.exe, powershell.exe, pwsh.exe, wmic.exe, tasklist.exe, netstat.exe, net.exe, net1.exe, ipconfig.exe, systeminfo.exe, whoami.exe, query.exe, ping.exe)) earliest=-1h@h latest=+1h@h by Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where discovery_events >= 5 ] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 30d;
let WindowMin = 60;
// 1. The signature: netsh interface portproxy relay creation
let PortProxy = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName =~ "netsh.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has "portproxy"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("add v4tov4", "add v4tov6", "add v6tov4", "add v6tov6")
    | project ProxyTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ProxyCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName;
// 2. Volt Typhoon's stereotyped discovery LOLBin burst on the same host within +/- 60m
let DiscoveryBins = dynamic(["tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","whoami.exe","query.exe","ping.exe","hostname.exe","nltest.exe"]);
let Discovery = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName in~ (DiscoveryBins)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wsmprovhost.exe")
    | project DiscTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DiscFile = FileName, DiscCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
PortProxy
| join kind=inner Discovery on DeviceId
| where DiscTime between (ProxyTime - WindowMin*1m .. ProxyTime + WindowMin*1m)
| summarize DiscoveryBinCount = dcount(DiscFile),
            DiscoveryBins = make_set(DiscFile, 25),
            DiscoverySamples = make_set(DiscCmd, 10),
            ProxyCmd = any(ProxyCmd),
            Parent = any(Parent),
            FirstSeen = min(DiscTime),
            LastSeen = max(DiscTime)
            by DeviceName, AccountName, ProxyTime
| where DiscoveryBinCount >= 3              // at least 3 distinct recon LOLBins around the relay
| order by ProxyTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 30d;
let WindowMin = 60;
let PortProxy = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where NewProcessName endswith "\\netsh.exe"
    | where CommandLine has "portproxy"
    | where CommandLine has_any ("add v4tov4","add v4tov6","add v6tov4","add v6tov6")
    | project ProxyTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ProxyCmd = CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
let DiscoveryBins = dynamic(["tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","whoami.exe","query.exe","ping.exe","hostname.exe","nltest.exe"]);
let Discovery = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where ChildBin in (DiscoveryBins)
    | where ParentProcessName has_any ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wsmprovhost.exe")
    | project DiscTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, ChildBin, DiscCmd = CommandLine;
PortProxy
| join kind=inner Discovery on Computer
| where DiscTime between (ProxyTime - WindowMin*1m .. ProxyTime + WindowMin*1m)
| summarize DiscoveryBinCount = dcount(ChildBin),
            DiscoveryBins = make_set(ChildBin, 25),
            DiscoverySamples = make_set(DiscCmd, 10),
            ProxyCmd = any(ProxyCmd),
            ParentProcessName = any(ParentProcessName)
            by Computer, Account, ProxyTime
| where DiscoveryBinCount >= 3
| order by ProxyTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Volt Typhoon netsh portproxy Relay Setup
id: b6f1d840-volt-netsh-portproxy
status: experimental
description: Detects creation of a netsh interface portproxy IPv4/IPv6 relay — Volt Typhoon's documented technique for pivoting from compromised internet-facing hosts into critical-infrastructure internal networks (CISA AA24-038A).
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-038a
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/05/24/volt-typhoon-targets-us-critical-infrastructure-with-living-off-the-land-techniques/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1090.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith: '\netsh.exe'
  selection_cmd:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'portproxy'
  selection_subcommand:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'add v4tov4'
      - 'add v4tov6'
      - 'add v6tov4'
      - 'add v6tov6'
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_image and selection_cmd and selection_subcommand and not filter_machine
falsepositives:
  - Authorized network/sysadmin troubleshooting — should be rare and ticketed; tier-0 admins on jump hosts only
level: high
ucs.rationale: Volt Typhoon's hallmark is using compromised SOHO/edge devices (KV-Botnet) plus on-host LOLBin pivoting to blend into normal admin traffic — they explicitly avoid commodity tooling like Cobalt Strike. CISA AA24-038A and Microsoft's threat profile both call out 'netsh interface portproxy add v4tov4' as a Volt-specific relay setup observed during 2023-2024 ICS/CNI intrusions. Combining it with the a
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
